title: cirsium eriophorum subsp. oviforme
intro: Cirsium eriophorum subsp. oviforme, also known as the woolly thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family, characterized by its distinctive woolly leaves and pink to purple flowers. Traditionally, the woolly thistle has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. Woolly thistle can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits.
